text:
Once upon a time in a small, sleepy town, there lived a bird named Rufus. Rufus was not like any other bird; he had a penchant for collecting forgotten souvenirs from the cobblestone streets. Every morning, he fluttered down from his perch on the old oak tree and scoured the town looking for odds and ends.

One particular winter, a big snowstorm swept through town, blanketing everything in silence and white. In this quiet snowstorm, Rufus came across an old brooch. It wasn't made of silver or gold—it was woven from thin threads of human memory. As he held it, memories of laughter, arguments, and quiet evenings filled his mind.

Curious, Rufus tucked the brooch under his plumage. He imagined holding it at parties, feeling the embrace of a loved one, or perhaps just wearing it on sunny days. It didn't feel like something he owned, but rather like a token of something he cherished.
